creator: glewis@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->feedback

I've got a few suggestions for some simplifications for this port.  Please 
review this shar file and let me know if you're ok with them.  The biggest 
changes are cutting down the packing list and simplify the install process. 

--- log4net.shar begins here --- 
# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before 
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may 
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and 
# have default permissions. 
# 
# This archive contains: 
# 
#	log4net 
#	log4net/files 
#	log4net/files/log4net.pc.in 
#	log4net/distinfo 
#	log4net/Makefile 
#	log4net/pkg-plist 
#	log4net/pkg-descr 
# 
echo c - log4net 
mkdir -p log4net > /dev/null 2>&1 
echo c - log4net/files 
mkdir -p log4net/files > /dev/null 2>&1 
echo x - log4net/files/log4net.pc.in 
sed 's/^X//' >log4net/files/log4net.pc.in << '01ed739c3e4436517f3a0b817d2962a7' 
Xprefix=%%PREFIX%% 
Xexec_prefix=${prefix} 
Xlibdir=${exec_prefix}/lib 
X 
XName: Log4net 
XDescription: Log4net - Logging library for C# and VB.NET 
XVersion: %%PORTVERSION%% 
XLibs: -r:${libdir}/mono/2.0/log4net.dll 
01ed739c3e4436517f3a0b817d2962a7 
echo x - log4net/distinfo 
sed 's/^X//' >log4net/distinfo << '9c4a556fe72b23c876ab1f249e859292' 
XMD5 (incubating-log4net-1.2.10.zip) = 0085970684888a5dfb1d59b2831f8d7e 
XSHA256 (incubating-log4net-1.2.10.zip) = 50f6124dd01febb7716625aced1727b3db200d589af2d84c99ee22d5ab34fb3f 
XSIZE (incubating-log4net-1.2.10.zip) = 8203576 
9c4a556fe72b23c876ab1f249e859292 
echo x - log4net/Makefile 
sed 's/^X//' >log4net/Makefile << '8a55f53985c016c620fe8ef3a6bfa035' 
X# New ports collection makefile for:		log4net 
X# Date created:					July 8th, 2005 
X# Whom:				Christopher Nehren <apeiron@coitusmentis.info> 
X# 
X# $FreeBSD$ 
X#   $Id: Makefile,v 1.13 2006/10/10 00:26:57 killfill Exp $ 
X# 
X 
XPORTNAME=	log4net 
XPORTVERSION=	1.2.10 
XCATEGORIES=	devel 
XMASTER_SITES=	APACHE 
XMASTER_SITE_SUBDIR=	incubator/log4net/${PORTVERSION} 
XDISTNAME=	incubating-${PORTNAME}-${PORTVERSION} 
X 
XMAINTAINER=	mono@FreeBSD.org 
XCOMMENT=	Port for .NET of the Apache Logging Services project 
X 
XBUILD_DEPENDS=	mono:${PORTSDIR}/lang/mono 
XRUN_DEPENDS=	mono:${PORTSDIR}/lang/mono 
X 
XWRKSRC=	${WRKDIR}/${PORTNAME}-${PORTVERSION} 
X 
XUSE_ZIP=	yes 
XNO_BUILD=	yes 
X 
XSUB_FILES=	log4net.pc 
XSUB_LIST+=	PORTVERSION=${PORTVERSION} 
X 
X.if !defined (NOPORTDOCS) 
XPORTDOCS=	* 
X.endif 
X.if !defined (NOPORTEXAMPLES) 
XPORTEXAMPLES=	* 
X.endif 
X 
Xdo-install: 
X	@(cd ${INSTALL_WRKSRC}/bin/mono/2.0/release && ${SETENV} MONO_SHARED_DIR=${WRKDIR} ${LOCALBASE}/bin/gacutil /root ${PREFIX}/lib/ /gacdir ${PREFIX}/lib /package 2.0 /i log4net.dll) 
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKDIR}/log4net.pc ${PREFIX}/libdata/pkgconfig/log4net.pc 
X.if !defined (NOPORTDOCS) 
X	@${ECHO_CMD} "Installing documentation..." 
X	@${MKDIR} ${DOCSDIR} 
X	@cd ${WRKSRC}/doc && ${COPYTREE_SHARE} . ${DOCSDIR} 
X.endif 
X.if !defined (NOPORTEXAMPLES) 
X	@${ECHO_CMD} "Installing examples..." 
X	@${MKDIR} ${EXAMPLESDIR} 
X	@cd ${WRKSRC}/examples && ${COPYTREE_SHARE} . ${EXAMPLESDIR} 
X.endif 
X 
X.include "${.CURDIR}/../../lang/mono/bsd.mono.mk" 
X.include <bsd.port.mk> 
8a55f53985c016c620fe8ef3a6bfa035 
echo x - log4net/pkg-plist 
sed 's/^X//' >log4net/pkg-plist << 'c180b17394661379857142fd3844c4df' 
Xlib/mono/2.0/log4net.dll 
Xlib/mono/gac/log4net/1.2.10.0__1b44e1d426115821/log4net.dll 
Xlibdata/pkgconfig/log4net.pc 
X@dirrm lib/mono/gac/log4net/1.2.10.0__1b44e1d426115821 
X@dirrm lib/mono/gac/log4net 
c180b17394661379857142fd3844c4df 
echo x - log4net/pkg-descr 
sed 's/^X//' >log4net/pkg-descr << '696c0a8f90134a6f41b605170e8dc2c7' 
Xlog4net is a tool to help the programmer output log statements to a variety of 
Xoutput targets. In case of problems with an application, it is helpful to 
Xenable logging so that the problem can be located. With log4net it is possible 
Xto enable logging at runtime without modifying the application binary. The 
Xlog4net package is designed so that log statements can remain in shipped code 
Xwithout incurring a high performance cost. It follows that the speed of logging 
X(or rather not logging) is crucial. 
X 
XAt the same time, log output can be so voluminous that it quickly becomes 
Xoverwhelming. One of the distinctive features of log4net is the notion of 
Xhierarchical loggers. Using these loggers it is possible to selectively control 
Xwhich log statements are output at arbitrary granularity. 
X 
Xlog4net is designed with two distinct goals in mind: speed and flexibility. 
XFeatures: 
X* Support for multiple frameworks 
X* Output to multiple logging targets 
X* Hierarchical logging architecture 
X* XML Configuration 
X* Dynamic Configuration 
X* Logging Context 
X* Proven architecture 
X* Modular and extensible design 
X* High performance with flexibility 
X 
XWWW: http://logging.apache.org/log4net/ 
696c0a8f90134a6f41b605170e8dc2c7 
exit 
--- log4net.shar ends here ---
